info.title: Aura API
info.description:
Public REST API for Aura — prediction markets on Alephium.

This API exposes everything a user can see or do in the app:
markets, prices, orderbook, trades, vault state, oracle and
governance, plus build-tx endpoints that return unsigned
transactions for the client to sign with its own wallet.

Interactive reference: https://docs.aurabets.io/api-reference/introduction

## Discovery

Built for integrators, not for lazy implementers:

- **Faceted lists** — every list endpoint accepts `?facets=true`
  and returns category / tag / status counts in the same response,
  so a UI can render filter chips without a second request.
- **Cursor pagination** — stable across inserts and fast at depth.
  Offset paging via `?page=` is still supported for compat.
- **Rich filters** — `?q=`, `?category=`, `?tags=`, `?status=`,
  `?creator=`, `?minVolume=` on `/v1/markets`. Tag matching is
  AND (all listed tags must be present).
- **Sortable** — `?sort=created-at|volume|best-bid-yes|recently-traded`
  with `?order=asc|desc`. Use `/v1/trending` for the composite-score sort.
- **Field projection** — `?fields=id,question,bestBidYes` trims the
  wire weight for thin clients.
- **Unified search** — `/v1/search?q=` runs a strict tsquery first
  with a fuzzy trigram fallback when nothing matches; result type is
  a discriminated union so adding events / users later is non-breaking.
- **Transparent trending** — `/v1/trending` returns the full
  algorithm + per-market component scores so the ranking is auditable.
- **Categories + tags as first-class** — `/v1/categories` and
  `/v1/tags` enumerate the taxonomy with market counts.

## Authentication

Aura uses passkey-backed account sessions delivered as an HttpOnly
cookie. Sign in by signing a one-time challenge with your passkey:

```
POST /v1/auth/challenge   { "address": "..." }
POST /v1/auth/session     { "address": "...", "publicKey": "...", "signature": "...", "passkey": { ... } }
```

The `session` call sets the `aura_session` cookie and returns a CSRF
token; send it back in the `x-csrf-token` header on every mutating
request. `POST /v1/auth/logout` clears the session.

Reads are accessible anonymously with low IP-based rate limits;
write endpoints (`/v1/tx/*`, social writes) require a valid session.

## Rate limits

| Tier      | Reads (req/min) | Writes (req/min) |
|-----------|----------------:|-----------------:|
| Anonymous | 100000 per IP | 100000 per IP |
| API key   | 100000 per key | 100000 per key |

Standard `RateLimit-*` response headers are returned on every
request (IETF `draft-ietf-httpapi-ratelimit-headers`).

## Write endpoints

Aura is non-custodial — the API never holds private keys.
Endpoints under `/v1/tx/*` return an *unsigned* transaction
that the caller must sign with its own wallet (passkey, mobile
wallet, hardware wallet, etc.) and submit to an Alephium node.

A typical "place order" flow:

1. `POST /v1/tx/markets/{address}/orders` → server returns
   `{ unsignedTx, gasEstimate, ... }`.
2. Wallet signs `unsignedTx`.
3. Wallet submits the signed tx to any Alephium node.

SessionCookie.name: aura_session
SessionCookie.description: HttpOnly session cookie set by POST /v1/auth/session. Mutating requests must also send the CSRF token (returned by that call) in the x-csrf-token header.

get.summary: Liveness probe.

post.summary: Mint a one-time signing challenge.
post.description: Returns a short text the wallet (passkey) must sign to prove ownership. The signed result is then submitted to `POST /v1/auth/session` to establish a session. `address` is optional: omit it for passkey login (where the address is only known after the assertion) to get a generic nonce, which the session call consumes by its exact text.

challenge.description: Plaintext message to sign with the wallet (signMessage with hasher="alephium").

post.summary: Sign in / register by submitting a signed challenge.
post.description: Verifies the passkey assertion against the most recent unconsumed challenge for the address, creates or refreshes the account row, and sets an HttpOnly session cookie bound to the verified wallet. The CSRF token is returned in the body (echo it back in the `x-csrf-token` header on subsequent mutating requests) and also set as a readable cookie.

publicKey.description: Public key whose hash equals `address` (33-byte compressed, hex).

signature.description: Signature produced by `signMessage(challenge, "alephium")`. The Alephium WebAuthn blob for passkey wallets.

keyType.description: Wallet key type. `gl-webauthn` = passkey (the only type the app uses).

passkey.description: Passkey credential metadata, persisted to the account row on first sign-in.

challenge.description: The exact challenge text the assertion signed. Send this for passkey login (where the challenge was issued without an address); the server consumes it by text. Omit it when the challenge was issued for a known address (it is then consumed by address).

refererAddress.description: Optional referrer wallet, captured once on first registration. Ignored if it equals the caller or does not exist.

csrf.description: CSRF token — send as the x-csrf-token header on mutating requests.

created.description: true when this call created a new account row.

get.summary: Return the current session (address + CSRF token).
get.description: Use on app load to check whether the session cookie is still valid and recover the CSRF token.

get.summary: Resolve a wallet from a passkey credential id.
get.description: Returns the public credential metadata (address + public keys + username) for a passkey credential id, read server-side. Used at login to resolve the wallet after a WebAuthn assertion, so the lookup does not depend on client-side state (which logout clears). Returning this metadata grants no session — sign-in still requires a valid assertion.

post.summary: Clear the session and CSRF cookies.
post.description: Always succeeds, even when there is no active session.

get.summary: List markets with full filter / sort / cursor / facet support.
get.description: The discovery workhorse. Use `?q=` for full-text search, `?category=` + `?tags=` for filtering, `?sort=` + `?order=` for ordering, and `?cursor=` for stable deep pagination (offset paging via `?page=` is also supported but not recommended past page 100). Pass `?facets=true` to get category / tag / status counts in the same response so a UI can render filter chips without a second request.

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: Full-text search across question + rules + sources + category + tags. PostgreSQL `websearch_to_tsquery` syntax.

parameters.name: tags
parameters.description: Comma-separated tag list. Markets must contain ALL listed tags.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque pagination cursor from a previous response.

parameters.name: facets
parameters.description: When true, include category/tag/status counts in the response.

parameters.name: fields
parameters.description: Comma-separated field projection. Allowed: id, marketAddress, question, category, tags, creator, resolved, finalOutcome, volume, bestBidYes, bestAskYes, bestBidNo, bestAskNo, createdAt, metadata, imageUrl, rules, resolutionSources, master, masterQuestion, masterImage, linked, proposalId.

id.description: Database id (stable, monotonic).

volume.description: Total traded volume in collateral base units (string, preserves precision).

page.description: Current page (offset mode only). Absent when paginating with `cursor`.

total.description: Total matching rows across all pages (offset mode only). Cursor mode omits for performance.

nextCursor.description: Opaque cursor for the next page. `null` when this page is the last one.

pagination.description: Pagination envelope returned alongside every list endpoint.

total.description: Total rows matching the base filter (independent of the dimension being faceted).

anyOf.description: Facet counts for the current filter set.

get.summary: Get a market by address.

id.description: Database id (stable, monotonic).

volume.description: Total traded volume in collateral base units (string, preserves precision).

get.summary: Aggregated order book for both outcomes.
get.description: Returns price-level aggregates for both YES and NO sides in a single response. Bids are highest-price-first; asks are lowest-price-first. The top-level `bestBid*` / `bestAsk*` fields summarise the touch.

totalQuantity.description: Sum of remaining quantity at this price (18-decimal string).
Sum of remaining quantity at this price (18-decimal string).
Sum of remaining quantity at this price (18-decimal string).
Sum of remaining quantity at this price (18-decimal string).

get.summary: Recent trades (executed fills) for a market.
get.description: Paginated stream of `order_fills`, newest first. Cursor pagination recommended for deep history (offset paging supported via `?page=` for compat).

fillType.description: How the fill came about: "limit", "market", "liquidation", etc.

page.description: Current page (offset mode only). Absent when paginating with `cursor`.

total.description: Total matching rows across all pages (offset mode only). Cursor mode omits for performance.

nextCursor.description: Opaque cursor for the next page. `null` when this page is the last one.

pagination.description: Pagination envelope returned alongside every list endpoint.

get.summary: Latest price snapshot for a market.
get.description: Returns the most recent `(yesPrice, priceTimestamp)` pair from `market_prices`. `noPrice` is computed as `1 - yesPrice`. Both prices are returned in [0, 1] probability.

priceTimestamp.description: Unix milliseconds when this price was observed.

get.summary: Bucketed price history for a market.
get.description: Defers to the `get_market_price_history` PL/pgSQL helper — bucket size depends on the requested `range`: 1m for 1H, 6m for 6H, 5m for 1D, 30m for 1W, 3h for 1M, 1d for ALL. Gaps are filled with the last-known price so the series renders smoothly in line charts.

get.summary: Markets in the same linked-market group.
get.description: Returns the master market + every leg in the linked group this market belongs to. 404s for standalone markets (no group exists) — call `/v1/markets/:address` instead. Use `/v1/events/:id` if you have the master id directly; this endpoint is for the "give me siblings of THIS market" lookup.

id.description: Database id (stable, monotonic).

volume.description: Total traded volume in collateral base units (string, preserves precision).

id.description: Database id (stable, monotonic).

volume.description: Total traded volume in collateral base units (string, preserves precision).

get.summary: List all known market categories with counts.
get.description: Counts reflect tradeable markets only (unresolved + master leg of any linked-market group). Pass the `slug` to /v1/markets?category=… to filter.

name.description: Display name. Currently == slug; may diverge later.

get.summary: List tags with market counts.
get.description: Returns the full tag list (or a substring-filtered subset) with the count of tradeable markets each tag is attached to. Pass `?category=` to scope the counts to one category.

get.summary: Unified search across markets (events + users coming).
get.description: Strict full-text search backed by Postgres `websearch_to_tsquery`. Falls back to fuzzy trigram matching when the strict query returns nothing (toggle with `?fuzzy=false`). Response is a discriminated union per-result-type so adding events / users later is non-breaking.

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: Query string (websearch syntax: AND, OR, NOT, "phrases").

parameters.name: types
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of resource types to include. Allowed: market. Defaults to all.

score.description: Higher = more relevant. Engine-dependent scale; do not compare across types.

id.description: Database id (stable, monotonic).

volume.description: Total traded volume in collateral base units (string, preserves precision).

fuzzyFallbackUsed.description: True when the strict tsquery returned 0 hits and we fell back to trigram fuzzy matching.

get.summary: Composite-score trending markets.
get.description: Top markets by a transparent blended score (volume × recency × tightness). The component scores are returned alongside each market so a UI can render "why is this trending" copy and integrators can audit the ranking.

parameters.name: halfLifeDays
parameters.description: Recency half-life in days. Lower = punishes older markets harder.

id.description: Database id (stable, monotonic).

volume.description: Total traded volume in collateral base units (string, preserves precision).

get.summary: List events (linked-market groups).
get.description: Each event is the master row of a linked-market group. `?withLegs=true` expands the full leg array on every event in the response (one extra DB query — keep it off when rendering an index page).

question.description: Master question shown in discovery (e.g. "NFL Week 5: Chiefs @ Eagles").

id.description: Database id (stable, monotonic).

volume.description: Total traded volume in collateral base units (string, preserves precision).

page.description: Current page (offset mode only). Absent when paginating with `cursor`.

total.description: Total matching rows across all pages (offset mode only). Cursor mode omits for performance.

nextCursor.description: Opaque cursor for the next page. `null` when this page is the last one.

pagination.description: Pagination envelope returned alongside every list endpoint.

get.summary: Get one event with its full leg array.
get.description: The `id` is the master market id (also surfaced as `event.id` on /v1/events). Always returns legs expanded — for a leg-less master view, query /v1/markets/:address directly using the master's address.

question.description: Master question shown in discovery (e.g. "NFL Week 5: Chiefs @ Eagles").

id.description: Database id (stable, monotonic).

volume.description: Total traded volume in collateral base units (string, preserves precision).

get.summary: Public profile + lifetime trading stats for a wallet.
get.description: Returns the trading + display columns from `users`. AI-quota, passkey, and vault-template columns are intentionally hidden — they are internal to the FE. Returns a synthesised zero-volume profile for never-traded addresses (no 404).

totalVolume.description: Lifetime trading volume (collateral base units string).

get.summary: Open + settled positions for a wallet.

page.description: Current page (offset mode only). Absent when paginating with `cursor`.

total.description: Total matching rows across all pages (offset mode only). Cursor mode omits for performance.

nextCursor.description: Opaque cursor for the next page. `null` when this page is the last one.

pagination.description: Pagination envelope returned alongside every list endpoint.

get.summary: Open / filled / cancelled orders for a wallet.
get.description: `status=open` filters to active book orders (not cancelled, not fully filled). `status=filled` returns orders where filled >= quantity. `status=cancelled` returns cancelled orders. `status=all` returns everything.

page.description: Current page (offset mode only). Absent when paginating with `cursor`.

total.description: Total matching rows across all pages (offset mode only). Cursor mode omits for performance.

nextCursor.description: Opaque cursor for the next page. `null` when this page is the last one.

pagination.description: Pagination envelope returned alongside every list endpoint.

get.summary: Parlay tickets for a wallet.
Combined "what does this wallet need to do right now" view.
get.description:
Aggregates three categories that demand user action:
  - **proposal-vote**: governance proposals in the active voting period that the wallet has not voted on.
  - **dispute-commit**: oracle disputes currently in the commit window the wallet has not committed on.
  - **dispute-reveal**: pending reveals queued for the wallet (the indexer relays them; this surfaces the queue).

Returned as a single discriminated union so a UI can render one badge count + one notifications drawer sorted by soonest deadline.

get.summary: Reveal-relay queue for this wallet (salt redacted).
get.description: Returns rows the indexer is tracking from `pending_reveals` for this voter. The `salt` column is intentionally NOT exposed — leaking the salt before reveal would let observers reconstruct the still-secret vote choice from the on-chain commit hash.

post.summary: Create (or refresh) the verified caller's user record.
post.description: Creates the `users` row for the authenticated wallet. The address is taken from the verified API key, never the body, so a caller can only ever create their own record. A referrer is captured once on first creation and only if it exists and differs from the caller. Idempotent.

refererAddress.description: Optional referrer wallet. Ignored if it equals the caller or does not exist.

created.description: true when this call inserted a new row, false when it already existed.
true when this call inserted a new row, false when it already existed.

get.summary: Top traders by composite (volume + win-ratio) score.
get.description: Backed by the `user_leaderboard{,_last_month,_total}` materialized views. Score = volume_rel * (0.7 + 0.3 * win_ratio); rank is `dense_rank()` over score DESC. Refresh cadence is owned by the indexer — clients should not assume sub-second freshness.

post.summary: Create a comment or reply as the verified caller.
Like a comment as the verified caller (idempotent).

delete.summary: Remove your like from a comment (idempotent).

post.summary: Create a comment or reply as the verified caller.
Like a comment as the verified caller (idempotent).

delete.summary: Remove your like from a comment (idempotent).

post.summary: Create a comment or reply as the verified caller.
Like a comment as the verified caller (idempotent).

delete.summary: Remove your like from a comment (idempotent).

get.summary: Current governance voting period state.
get.description: Returns the active voting period (one window during which proposals are votable) plus a convenience `isOpen` flag and the server `now`. Clients should use `now` (not their local clock) for any countdown UI to avoid skew complaints from end-users.

current.description: The active voting period, or null if none.

get.summary: List governance proposals.
get.description: Filter by `status`, `category`, `proposer`, or `activePeriodOnly=true`. Use `?sort=` to order by created-at, vote-count, or unique-voters. Cursor pagination via `?cursor=`; offset paging via `?page=` is supported but not recommended past page 100.

voteCount.description: Sum of vote weights as string (preserves bigint precision).

page.description: Current page (offset mode only). Absent when paginating with `cursor`.

total.description: Total matching rows across all pages (offset mode only). Cursor mode omits for performance.

nextCursor.description: Opaque cursor for the next page. `null` when this page is the last one.

pagination.description: Pagination envelope returned alongside every list endpoint.

get.summary: Get a single proposal by id.

voteCount.description: Sum of vote weights as string (preserves bigint precision).

get.summary: List votes cast on a proposal.

weight.description: Vote weight (string to preserve bigint precision).

post.summary: Dry-run validate a market proposal before signing.
post.description: Pure validator — no chain, no DB, no signer. Returns a list of issues + per-field UTF-8 byte sizes + the estimated ALPH attached to the eventual tx. Use this from a "preview" screen so users see "your proposal looks good" (or what to fix) before being asked to sign anything.

post.summary: Create a new proposal draft for the calling wallet.
post.description: Drafts are wallet-private; the verified session's wallet address is used as `user_address`. Every field is optional — drafts can be partially filled at any point.

marketOptions.description: Per-leg questions for categorical / linked markets.

get.summary: List the calling wallet's drafts.
get.description: Newest first. No pagination yet — the practical cap on personal drafts is small.

marketOptions.description: Per-leg questions for categorical / linked markets.

get.summary: Fetch a single draft by id (must be owned by the calling wallet).

marketOptions.description: Per-leg questions for categorical / linked markets.

patch.summary: Update fields on an existing draft.
patch.description: Partial update — only the fields present in the request body are written. Returns the full draft after the update applies.

marketOptions.description: Per-leg questions for categorical / linked markets.

get.summary: List disputed markets, with derived phase.
get.description: Filter by `?phase=` (commit-open / reveal-open / awaiting-finalization / finalized) to scope to actionable rows. The `phase` is computed server-side from the raw commit/reveal timestamps + finalised flag, so every consumer sees the same answer.

parameters.name: phase
parameters.description: Filter by computed phase (commit-open, reveal-open, etc).

page.description: Current page (offset mode only). Absent when paginating with `cursor`.

total.description: Total matching rows across all pages (offset mode only). Cursor mode omits for performance.

nextCursor.description: Opaque cursor for the next page. `null` when this page is the last one.

pagination.description: Pagination envelope returned alongside every list endpoint.

get.summary: Get the dispute state for a specific market.
List revealed votes on a disputed market.
get.description: Returns the `dispute_votes` rows for the latest `vote_round` of the dispute on this market. Pre-reveal commits are NOT exposed (that data leaks the still-secret vote).

post.summary: Submit a pending reveal payload after committing a dispute vote.
post.description: Right after a user signs `commitVote`, post the matching `{salt, voteChoice}` here. The indexer's reveal-automation will submit the on-chain `revealVote` tx during the reveal window. Authentication is implicit — the body must hash to the on-chain commit hash.

post.summary: Record a Sponsor Market Rewards deposit.
post.description: Call AFTER transferring USDT to the treasury wallet. The deposit is verified on-chain (server-side) before any maker is ever paid: the row starts `pending` and the indexer activates it once the tx is confirmed to have transferred USDT to the treasury. Idempotent on `txId`.

txId.description: On-chain tx id of the USDT transfer to the treasury.

totalAmount.description: Verified on-chain USDT amount (decimal string). "0" until verified.
Verified on-chain USDT amount (decimal string). "0" until verified.

get.summary: List the calling wallet's referral payouts.
get.description:
Returns the referral-fee payouts owed to / paid to the authenticated wallet, newest first. Sourced from the service-role-only `payout_queue` table (`category = referral_fees`, scoped to the caller). Each row carries the amount (base units + USDT decimal string), the lifecycle status, the submit/confirm tx ids, the originating market, and timestamps.

Legitimately empty until referred trades settle on-chain and the payout worker enqueues rows — an empty list is the normal state, not an error.

amountBaseUnits.description: Payout amount in chain-native base units (18 decimals), as a string.

amount.description: Payout amount as a USDT decimal string (base units / 1e18).

submitTxId.description: On-chain tx that submitted the payout.

marketId.description: Market the referral fee came from.

post.summary: Add a market to the caller's watchlist.
post.description: Upserts a watchlist row for the verified session wallet. Idempotent on (wallet, market). The acting wallet is derived from the session, never the request body.

delete.summary: Remove a market from the caller's watchlist.
delete.description: Deletes the watchlist row for the verified session wallet + market. Idempotent.

patch.summary: Mark one of the caller's notifications read.

post.summary: Mark all of the caller's notifications read.

delete.summary: Delete one of the caller's notifications.
Delete all of the caller's notifications.

get.summary: Identify the currently-logged-in admin.
get.description: Returns the verified admin wallet address from the passkey session. Used by the FE to render the top-bar and to confirm the session is still valid + allowlisted before letting an admin start a flow.

get.summary: List recent admin audit entries.

post.summary: Issue a wallet step-up challenge for an admin action.
post.description: Returns a text the admin's wallet must sign to authorize a specific action with specific parameters. The signed proof is later submitted to `POST /v1/admin/actions/<verb>`.

post.summary: Authorize a "start voting period" action.
post.description: Verifies a step-up wallet signature for `voting.start_period`, records the authorization in the admin audit log, and returns the challengeId so the FE can reference it when reporting the on-chain tx outcome.

publicKey.description: Hex string, with or without `0x` prefix.

signature.description: Hex string, with or without `0x` prefix.

post.summary: Report the on-chain outcome of a previously-authorized action.
post.description: Appends an outcome entry to the audit log referencing a previously-issued step-up challenge. Used after the FE has broadcast the tx the admin authorized.

get.summary: Hero KPIs for the admin Overview page.
get.description: Wraps the `admin_overview()` SQL function. Returns market counts, 24h trading volume, user counts, indexer health summary, and open bug-report count in a single round-trip.

get.summary: Current state of every indexer heartbeat.
Currently-open incidents across the system.
get.description: Reads the admin_incidents view: stale heartbeats, stuck markets, and pending reveals older than 30 minutes.

get.summary: List bug reports.

parameters.name: since
parameters.description: ISO-8601; only reports created at-or-after this time

get.summary: Fetch a single bug report.

post.summary: Mark a bug report as resolved (or update its triage status).
post.description: Updates the report's status + records the operator + note. No step-up required — bug-report triage has no contract impact, just an audit log entry.

get.summary: List markets with admin-only fields (status, stuck flag, dispute timestamps).
Full admin drill-in for a single market.
get.description: Wraps admin_market_detail(): returns market metadata, status + stuck flag, recent fills (50), holders summary + top-20 holders, dispute state if disputed, settlement state if settled, and outstanding payout-queue counts.

get.summary: Bucketed trading time series (trade count + volume).
Top markets by volume in the requested window.
List users with admin-only fields.
Bucketed new-user signups + active distinct traders.
Top traders by volume in the requested window.
Referral leaderboard.
Oracle dashboard hero KPIs.
Active and recent disputes with phase + remaining time.
Pending reveal queue.
Liquidity rewards overview: current epoch + lifetime totals + tier config.
Recent epochs.
Recent payouts.
Top makers by lifetime reward earnings.

post.summary: Force the indexer to retry settlement for a stuck market.
post.description: Resets every payout_queue row for the target market back to status="pending" with attempts=0 and last_error=null. The indexer's payoutWorker picks them up on its next tick (~30s). Step-up signing required because this can re-broadcast on-chain transactions.

publicKey.description: Hex string, with or without `0x` prefix.

signature.description: Hex string, with or without `0x` prefix.

post.summary: Edit admin-only market metadata (shadow-ban, triage note).
post.description: Upserts admin_market_metadata for the target market. Step-up signing required because the shadow-ban flag is exposed to the public FE.

publicKey.description: Hex string, with or without `0x` prefix.

signature.description: Hex string, with or without `0x` prefix.

post.summary: Retry a stuck pending_reveals row.
post.description: Resets the row's status to "pending" with attempt_count=0 and last_error=null so the indexer's oracleReveals loop re-attempts on its next tick.

publicKey.description: Hex string, with or without `0x` prefix.

signature.description: Hex string, with or without `0x` prefix.

post.summary: Authorize a contract pause/unpause (kill switch).
post.description: Verifies a step-up signature, records the intent in the audit log at CRITICAL severity, and alerts Telegram. The actual on-chain `setPaused()` call is performed by the FE using the same wallet — this endpoint is the audit + auth layer.

publicKey.description: Hex string, with or without `0x` prefix.

signature.description: Hex string, with or without `0x` prefix.

post.summary: Build an unsigned PlaceLimitOrder transaction.
post.description: Returns an unsigned tx the client can sign. For BUY orders the response includes the exact USDT collateral the user must hold (price × quantity / 1000 + 2.5% fee).

signerPublicKey.description: Signer's public key (33-byte secp256k1 hex). Required for the node to build the witness layout.

signerKeyType.description: Defaults to "default" (groupless secp256k1). Pass "gl-webauthn" for passkey wallets.

quantity.description: Quantity in 18-decimal share base units (string to preserve precision).

unsignedTx.description: Hex-encoded unsigned transaction. Sign and submit via your wallet.

id.description: Hex string, with or without `0x` prefix.

post.summary: Build an unsigned PlaceMarketOrder transaction.
post.description: Market orders cross the book at the best available price. `expectedPrice` (optional) tightens the pre-locked collateral; if omitted, MAX_ORDER_PRICE is used (worst case) and the contract refunds unused collateral at fill time.

signerPublicKey.description: Signer's public key (33-byte secp256k1 hex). Required for the node to build the witness layout.

signerKeyType.description: Defaults to "default" (groupless secp256k1). Pass "gl-webauthn" for passkey wallets.

quantity.description: Quantity in 18-decimal share base units (string to preserve precision).

unsignedTx.description: Hex-encoded unsigned transaction. Sign and submit via your wallet.

id.description: Hex string, with or without `0x` prefix.

post.summary: Build an unsigned CancelOrder transaction.

signerPublicKey.description: Signer's public key (33-byte secp256k1 hex). Required for the node to build the witness layout.

signerKeyType.description: Defaults to "default" (groupless secp256k1). Pass "gl-webauthn" for passkey wallets.

unsignedTx.description: Hex-encoded unsigned transaction. Sign and submit via your wallet.

id.description: Hex string, with or without `0x` prefix.

post.summary: Build an unsigned vault deposit transaction (lock AURA).

signerPublicKey.description: Signer's public key (33-byte secp256k1 hex). Required for the node to build the witness layout.

signerKeyType.description: Defaults to "default" (groupless secp256k1). Pass "gl-webauthn" for passkey wallets.

amount.description: Amount in token base units (string to preserve precision).

unsignedTx.description: Hex-encoded unsigned transaction. Sign and submit via your wallet.

id.description: Hex string, with or without `0x` prefix.

post.summary: Build an unsigned vault unlock-request transaction.
post.description: Starts the unlock timelock (default 7 days). Withdraw only succeeds after the timelock elapses.

signerPublicKey.description: Signer's public key (33-byte secp256k1 hex). Required for the node to build the witness layout.

signerKeyType.description: Defaults to "default" (groupless secp256k1). Pass "gl-webauthn" for passkey wallets.

unlockAmount.description: Amount in token base units (string to preserve precision).

unsignedTx.description: Hex-encoded unsigned transaction. Sign and submit via your wallet.

id.description: Hex string, with or without `0x` prefix.

post.summary: Build an unsigned vault withdraw transaction (after unlock timelock).

signerPublicKey.description: Signer's public key (33-byte secp256k1 hex). Required for the node to build the witness layout.

signerKeyType.description: Defaults to "default" (groupless secp256k1). Pass "gl-webauthn" for passkey wallets.

unsignedTx.description: Hex-encoded unsigned transaction. Sign and submit via your wallet.

id.description: Hex string, with or without `0x` prefix.

post.summary: Build an unsigned OptimisticOracle.submitOutcome transaction.
post.description: Locks the on-chain propose bond (USDT) alongside the proposed outcome. The bond size is read from the oracle contract server-side; `bondAmount` is optional and, when sent, must match it exactly.

signerPublicKey.description: Signer's public key (33-byte secp256k1 hex). Required for the node to build the witness layout.

signerKeyType.description: Defaults to "default" (groupless secp256k1). Pass "gl-webauthn" for passkey wallets.

bondAmount.description: Amount in token base units (string to preserve precision).

unsignedTx.description: Hex-encoded unsigned transaction. Sign and submit via your wallet.

id.description: Hex string, with or without `0x` prefix.

post.summary: Build an unsigned OptimisticOracle.disputeOutcome transaction.
post.description: Locks the on-chain dispute bond (USDT) to challenge the proposed outcome. The bond size is read from the oracle contract server-side; `bondAmount` is optional and, when sent, must match it exactly.

signerPublicKey.description: Signer's public key (33-byte secp256k1 hex). Required for the node to build the witness layout.

signerKeyType.description: Defaults to "default" (groupless secp256k1). Pass "gl-webauthn" for passkey wallets.

bondAmount.description: Amount in token base units (string to preserve precision).

unsignedTx.description: Hex-encoded unsigned transaction. Sign and submit via your wallet.

id.description: Hex string, with or without `0x` prefix.

post.summary: Build an unsigned OptimisticOracle.commitVote transaction.
post.description: Posts the commit hash on-chain. The full reveal payload should ALSO be posted to /v1/oracle/pending-reveal so the indexer's reveal-automation can dispatch the reveal during the reveal window.

signerPublicKey.description: Signer's public key (33-byte secp256k1 hex). Required for the node to build the witness layout.

signerKeyType.description: Defaults to "default" (groupless secp256k1). Pass "gl-webauthn" for passkey wallets.

unsignedTx.description: Hex-encoded unsigned transaction. Sign and submit via your wallet.

id.description: Hex string, with or without `0x` prefix.

post.summary: Build an unsigned OptimisticOracle.revealVote transaction.
post.description: Manually reveal a previously-committed vote. Most callers should rely on the indexer's reveal-automation (post the payload to /v1/oracle/pending-reveal); this endpoint is for self-service reveals.

signerPublicKey.description: Signer's public key (33-byte secp256k1 hex). Required for the node to build the witness layout.

signerKeyType.description: Defaults to "default" (groupless secp256k1). Pass "gl-webauthn" for passkey wallets.

unsignedTx.description: Hex-encoded unsigned transaction. Sign and submit via your wallet.

id.description: Hex string, with or without `0x` prefix.

post.summary: Build an unsigned Voting.vote transaction.
post.description: Casts a governance vote on a proposal. Voting weight is read from on-chain stake.

signerPublicKey.description: Signer's public key (33-byte secp256k1 hex). Required for the node to build the witness layout.

signerKeyType.description: Defaults to "default" (groupless secp256k1). Pass "gl-webauthn" for passkey wallets.

unsignedTx.description: Hex-encoded unsigned transaction. Sign and submit via your wallet.

id.description: Hex string, with or without `0x` prefix.

post.summary: Build an unsigned ProposeAndSubmitMarket transaction (single-leg).
post.description: Submits one binary (yes/no) market proposal for governance review in a single tx. For a multi-outcome event (e.g. "who wins this game?" with 4 candidates), use POST /v1/tx/factory/propose-linked-market instead — it bundles all legs into one signed tx. Strings are accepted as plain text and hex-encoded internally.

signerPublicKey.description: Signer's public key (33-byte secp256k1 hex). Required for the node to build the witness layout.

signerKeyType.description: Defaults to "default" (groupless secp256k1). Pass "gl-webauthn" for passkey wallets.

unsignedTx.description: Hex-encoded unsigned transaction. Sign and submit via your wallet.

id.description: Hex string, with or without `0x` prefix.

post.summary: Build an unsigned multi-leg market proposal (one tx, N legs).
post.description:
The right primitive for any market with more than two outcomes — sports games, multi-candidate races, conditional markets. Provide an array of `legs[]` with their individual question/imageUrl/metadata + the shared masterQuestion / category / rules / sources. The endpoint picks the right ProposeAndSubmitMarketLinked{N} script (2..40 legs) and returns ONE unsigned tx covering all proposals.

The total ALPH attached scales linearly with the leg count (PROPOSAL_DEPOSIT + DUST per leg). The `legCount` field in the response confirms which Linked{N} script was selected.

signerPublicKey.description: Signer's public key (33-byte secp256k1 hex). Required for the node to build the witness layout.

signerKeyType.description: Defaults to "default" (groupless secp256k1). Pass "gl-webauthn" for passkey wallets.

masterQuestion.description: Display question shown in markets-list UIs as the parent event row.

imageUrl.description: Per-leg image URL. Empty defers to masterImageUrl.

unsignedTx.description: Hex-encoded unsigned transaction. Sign and submit via your wallet.

id.description: Hex string, with or without `0x` prefix.

post.summary: Build an unsigned MarketFactory.parlayDeposit transaction.
post.description: Locks `amount` USDT and submits a parlay across multiple markets. `marketIds` must be pre-encoded as hex per the contract's expected format.

signerPublicKey.description: Signer's public key (33-byte secp256k1 hex). Required for the node to build the witness layout.

signerKeyType.description: Defaults to "default" (groupless secp256k1). Pass "gl-webauthn" for passkey wallets.

amount.description: Amount in token base units (string to preserve precision).

combinedOdds.description: Amount in token base units (string to preserve precision).

marketIds.description: Hex string, with or without `0x` prefix.

unsignedTx.description: Hex-encoded unsigned transaction. Sign and submit via your wallet.

id.description: Hex string, with or without `0x` prefix.

post.summary: Build an unsigned MarketFactory.linkProposals transaction (admin-flavoured).
post.description: Groups already-on-chain proposals into one event with a master question + image. **For most users, prefer POST /v1/tx/factory/propose-linked-market** — it proposes all legs AND links them in a single signed tx, which is the right UX for "I want to create a multi-outcome market". This endpoint is the lower-level operation, useful when legs were proposed independently (e.g. by a sports bot) and need to be linked after the fact.

signerPublicKey.description: Signer's public key (33-byte secp256k1 hex). Required for the node to build the witness layout.

signerKeyType.description: Defaults to "default" (groupless secp256k1). Pass "gl-webauthn" for passkey wallets.

linkedIds.description: Hex-encoded list of leg proposal ids.

unsignedTx.description: Hex-encoded unsigned transaction. Sign and submit via your wallet.

id.description: Hex string, with or without `0x` prefix.
